name: pressure_fed_lox_kerosene_system_c88e7b74
description: Design a pressure-fed rocket engine feed system using LOX (liquid oxygen) and kerosene as propellants, with nitrogen (GN2) as the pressurant gas. The system must include: GN2 tank for pressurization, LOX tank, kerosene tank, a rocket engine block, pressurization lines from GN2 to each propellant tank, and feed lines from each tank to the engine. FIXED: component architecture (GN2 tank, LOX tank, kerosene tank, engine, pressurization lines, feed lines). TUNABLE: tank pressures, volumes, line dimensions, flow rates, and timing parameters. The system must establish stable flow through both propellant feed lines to the engine.

notes: Use GN2 to pressurize both LOX and kerosene tanks
Ensure both propellants flow to the engine
Follow pressure-fed architecture with no turbopumps
Preserve the Engine node and the two feed Series connections into it.
Tune feed CdA values before changing topology.
If physical warnings mention a component, fix that component first.

checks.description: Simulation completed successfully without crash or invalid configuration
No nonphysical or degenerate warnings during simulation
LOX feed line delivers propellant to engine (nonzero mass flow)
Kerosene feed line delivers propellant to engine (nonzero mass flow)
GN2 pressurization line to LOX tank active (nonzero mass flow)
GN2 pressurization line to kerosene tank active (nonzero mass flow)
LOX feed line delivers non-zero mass flow to the engine.
Kerosene feed line delivers non-zero mass flow to the engine.
